children.heading: Reservations for eligible States and units of local government
children.label: a Reservations for eligible States and units of local government

children.text: The Secretary shall reserve one-third of the amounts appropriated pursuant to authorization under for each calendar quarter for the purpose of making payments to eligible State governments under subsection (b).section 6722 of this title
The Secretary shall reserve two-thirds of such amounts for the purpose of making payments to eligible units of local government under subsection (c).

The Secretary shall allocate from amounts reserved under subsection (a)(1) an amount for the purpose of making payments to each State equal to the total amount reserved under subsection (a)(1) for the calendar quarter multiplied by the applicable State percentage.

children.label: A
children.text: the State excess unemployment percentage, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the State revenue sharing amount by the sum of such products for all the States.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “State” means each State of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: the State excess unemployment percentage is equal to the difference resulting from the subtraction of 4.5 percentage points from the State unemployment rate for that State but shall not be less than zero;

children.label: C
children.text: the State unemployment rate is equal to the rate of unemployment in the State during the appropriate calendar quarter, as determined by the Secretary of Labor and reported to the Secretary; and

children.label: D
children.text: the State revenue sharing amount is the amount determined under sections 6705–6707(a) of title 31 for the most recently completed entitlement period, as defined under .1section 6701(a)(1) of title 31See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Local government allocation; percentage; definitions; special limitation
children.label: c Local government allocation; percentage; definitions; special limitation

children.text: The Secretary shall allocate from amounts reserved under subsection (a)(2) an amount for the purpose of making payments to each local government, subject to the provisions of paragraph (4), equal to the total amount reserved under such subsection for calendar quarter multiplied by the local government percentage.

children.label: A
children.text: the local excess unemployment percentage, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the local revenue sharing amount, by the sum of such products for all local governments.

children.label: A
children.text: the local excess unemployment percentage is equal to the difference resulting from the subtraction of 4.5 percentage points from the local unemployment rate, but shall not be less than zero;

children.label: i
children.text: the Governor of the State in which such local government is located has provided the Secretary of Labor with a local unemployment rate for such local government, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary of Labor finds that such local unemployment rate provided by the Governor has been determined in a manner consistent with the procedures and methodologies used by the Secretary of Labor in determining local unemployment rates,

children.label: C
children.text: the local revenue sharing amount is the amount determined under sections 6701(a)(5), (7), (b)–(d), and 6708–6712 of title 31 for the most recently completed entitlement period, as defined under ;11section 6701(a)(1) of title 31

children.label: i
children.text: is a unit of general government (determined on the basis of the same principles as are used by the Bureau of the Census for general statistical purposes), and

children.label: ii
children.text: performs substantial governmental functions. Such term includes the District of Columbia and also includes the recognized governing body of an Indian tribe or Alaskan Native village which performs substantial governmental functions. Such term does not include the government of a township area unless such government performs substantial governmental functions.

If the amount which would be allocated to any unit of local government under this subsection is less than $100, then no amount shall be allocated for such unit of local government under this subsection.
